target: F64 scalar/tensor broadcast add / sub / mul / div (both operand orders)

lever.description: Add an F64 scalar/tensor broadcast fast path (eval_f64_scalar_broadcast_binop + monomorphized f64_scalar_broadcast_map) to the Scalar⊗Tensor and Tensor⊗Scalar branches of eval_binary_elementwise, covering Add/Sub/Mul/Div. Iterates the F64 tensor with a tight inlined f64 op loop using the broadcast scalar, with a scalar_on_left flag to preserve operand order for non-commutative Sub/Div. Skips the per-element enum/promotion dispatch of binary_literal_op. Non-F64 scalar/elements and non-Add/Sub/Mul/Div primitives return None and fall through to the generic path.

baseline.note: fast-path guards temporarily removed; generic binary_literal_op path

after.tool: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'eval/(scalar_mul|tensor_sub_scalar)_1k_f64_vec'

isomorphism_proof.ordering: Output elements produced in tensor index order; scalar_on_left preserves Scalar⊗Tensor vs Tensor⊗Scalar operand order for Sub/Div.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Each output bit is generated by the same scalar f64 +,-,*,/ expression used by the generic path (lib.rs closures), then stored with to_bits.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: No comparisons or tie-breaking are introduced.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.errors: Only F64 scalar broadcast over F64 tensors for Add/Sub/Mul/Div take the fast path; other dtypes/primitives/malformed literals return None and use the generic path.
